prompt: Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print composition. A lone traveller in traditional robes descends a steep layered mountain whose contoured ridges form a bowl-like valley, rendered as flat stacked planes of unmodulated indigo, ochre, and muted vermillion with no shading or cast shadows. The path curves along the steepest slope toward the deepest point of the valley far below. Multiple false valleys are visible on flanking ridges, each shallower than the central descent. Bold black keyblock outlines define every edge. Background sky filled with flat Prussian blue. Decorative cloud bands in the upper corners rendered as flat white curves. Visible woodgrain texture in the sky and flat color areas. Asymmetric composition with strong diagonal from upper right to lower left. No text, no numbers, no Western perspective, no photographic lighting, no gradients, no blending of colors.
tradition_approach: Flat unmodulated color planes separated by bold keyblock outlines, Hokusai-style layered mountain composition, Prussian blue and ochre palette, visible woodgrain texture, asymmetric diagonal structure, zero Western shading or perspective.
